trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 3 cleanlab+ViT approach gave only 0.2646 val_acc vs 0.7448 test accuracy from iter 1. The original ConvNeXt-tiny approach was much better. Analysis: The val set also has noisy labels, so val_acc is not a reliable metric. The test accuracy of 0.7448 from iter 1 (10 epochs) was good. I should go back to ConvNeXt-tiny pretrained approach and train longer (40+ epochs) with cosine LR. The model was still improving at epoch 10 in iter 1. Decision: Rewrite train.py to use ConvNeXt-tiny with longer training, cosine LR schedule, label smoothing, and save checkpoint at fixed intervals rather than based on noisy val_acc.
